data.title: Bitcoin Network Rune DEX Platform OdinSwap Announces It Will Gradually Stop Operations
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bitcoin network Rune DEX platform OdinSwap announced that it and Rune startup platform Valhalla will gradually cease operations, and all user funds are safe. OdinSwap and Valhalla will continue to operate until February 28, 2025. Users are advised to withdraw funds before February 28. After February 28, any unclaimed balance will be manually refunded, but the appropriate BTC network fee will be paid. Refunds will be processed manually and may take up to one month to complete. Only balances that exceed the network fee will be refunded.

data.title: Mansa Secures $10 Million Seed Funding Led By Tether
data.description: According to PANews, stablecoin liquidity provider Mansa has announced the completion of a $10 million seed funding round, led by Tether. The funding includes both equity and debt financing, with Tether contributing $3 million in equity. The newly acquired funds will aid Mansa's expansion into Latin America and Southeast Asia, regions facing liquidity challenges that also hinder cross-border transactions.

data.title: Stablecoin liquidity provider Mansa completes $10 million seed round of financing, led by Tether
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Mansa, a stablecoin liquidity provider focusing on the African market, announced the completion of a $10 million seed round of financing. Mansa's stablecoin-based solution focuses on providing payment providers in the market with a flexible and reliable way to manage liquidity issues in cross-border payments. This financing includes a $3 million Preseed round of financing led by Tether and co-led by Polymorphic Capital, with participation from other well-known investors such as Octerra Capital, Faculty Group and Trive Digital. In addition, the company has also obtained an additional $7 million in liquidity funds from institutions including corporate investors, quantitative funds and alternative investment institutions.

data.title: Over 17,000 MKR Tokens Burned in Recent Transaction
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert has reported that approximately one minute ago, 17,362 MKR tokens were destroyed from an unknown wallet. The value of the burned tokens is estimated at $20,210,634.

data.title: 17,362 MKR were destroyed in an unknown wallet, equivalent to $20.21 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Whale Alert monitoring, about 5 minutes ago, 17,362 MKRs were destroyed in an unknown wallet, equivalent to approximately US$20.21 million.

(This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Fidelity Acquires Significant Ethereum Holdings
data.description: According to PANews, Fidelity's spot Ethereum ETF purchased 9,170 ETH on February 19, valued at $24.5 million, as reported by Cointelegraph.

data.title: Trump Increases Oversight on Independent Federal Agencies
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. President Donald Trump is set to enhance control over independent federal agencies, marking a significant move to strengthen his administration's influence over government bodies. 

In a statement from the White House, it was announced that Trump signed an executive order late Tuesday aimed at 'constraining independent agencies.' This order mandates these agencies to submit draft regulations for review and establishes 'performance standards' that require agencies to consult with the White House on 'priorities and strategic plans.' 

The executive order targets agencies such as the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC), and the Federal Communications Commission (FCC). While the Federal Reserve's monetary policy functions remain unaffected, its regulatory functions are subject to the new order.

data.title: Former Bybit payroll manager who stole $4.2 million sentenced to six weeks in prison for contempt of court
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Ho Kai Xin, the former Bybit payroll manager who stole $4.2 million in public funds, refused to execute the court order and spent nearly $840,000 to buy a freehold penthouse and luxury goods after the court's ruling. Due to violating the High Court's order, the woman was sentenced to six weeks in prison for contempt of court on January 27.
Earlier news, Bybit sued Ms. Ho, who was in charge of the company's payroll, for abusing her power and transferring a large amount of USDT to an address she secretly owned and controlled. The General Division of the Singapore High Court upheld the ruling on July 25, saying that cryptocurrencies are generally recognized as property, and holders of crypto assets in principle have intangible property rights recognized by common law, as items in litigation, and therefore can be enforced in court. The court ruled that Ms. Ho immediately pay Bybit all transferred amounts and interest. (straitstimes)

data.title: A new wallet address participating in the PAIN pre-sale has a floating profit of 2.3 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a newly created address previously withdrew 20,000 SOL (about 4.3 million US dollars) from Binance to participate in the PAIN pre-sale. Received a refund of 215,328 PAIN (about 3.85 million US dollars) and 16,000 SOL (about 2.75 million US dollars), and now has a floating profit of 2.3 million US dollars.
